content.text:
Root cause found. In `src/paginate.js:5`, the slice is `all.slice(start, start + size - 1)` — the `- 1` drops one item per page. Meanwhile `summary.js` computes `last = page * size` (correct), so the label claims 10 rows but only 9 render.

Let me confirm the baseline test state, then add a failing test that reproduces the bug before fixing.

content.text: Failing test confirms the root cause. Now the single fix — remove the erroneous `- 1` from the slice bound.

input.old_string: return { items: all.slice(start, start + size - 1), total: all.length };
input.new_string: return { items: all.slice(start, start + size), total: all.length };

lines: // The last page may be short; a page past the end yields empty items.
-  return { items: all.slice(start, start + size - 1), total: all.length };
+  return { items: all.slice(start, start + size), total: all.length };

content.text:
Fixed. All 3 tests pass.

**Root cause:** `src/paginate.js:5` used `all.slice(start, start + size - 1)`. `Array.slice(begin, end)` is end-exclusive, so subtracting 1 dropped the last row of every page — returning 9 items for a size-10 page. Meanwhile `summary.js` correctly computes `last = page * size`, so the label said "1-10" while only 9 rows rendered.

**Fix:** removed the stray `- 1` so the slice bound is `start + size`. This also correctly handles the short last page (e.g. page 3 of 23 now returns all 3 remaining rows), verified by the added test.

result:
Fixed. All 3 tests pass.

**Root cause:** `src/paginate.js:5` used `all.slice(start, start + size - 1)`. `Array.slice(begin, end)` is end-exclusive, so subtracting 1 dropped the last row of every page — returning 9 items for a size-10 page. Meanwhile `summary.js` correctly computes `last = page * size`, so the label said "1-10" while only 9 rows rendered.

**Fix:** removed the stray `- 1` so the slice bound is `start + size`. This also correctly handles the short last page (e.g. page 3 of 23 now returns all 3 remaining rows), verified by the added test.
